title: Pre-Determined and Re-Adjusted Stances — empty self-audit template
html:
TL;DR

  • Feb 1, 2023 self-audit skeleton — header "Rules that govern your thinking" with 18 empty topic slots: Dad, Mom, Sister, Brother, Sexuality, Girls, Love, Computer Science, Anime, Politics, Mexican-American, Idols, Meta-Thinking, Slave to responsibility, Future Career, Entrepreneurship, Hobbies, Depression, Book/Reading, Life.
  • Each slot is blank — no content underneath any heading. Captured the structure but never filled it.
  • Sits at the start of OSU sophomore winter term — same window as the Patrick Mooney prep and the Occam internship runup.
  • The slot list itself is a fingerprint: what 21-year-old Yahir thought were the piercing prompt categories worth committing personal stances on.
  • "Sister" is listed even though Yahir doesn't have a sister — likely template inheritance; flagged for brother disambiguation in densify.

Notes

This is an artifact — value is in the taxonomy he chose, not any actual stance content. The same impulse shows up again in the year recap format he later uses heavily — Yahir likes a numbered template even when he doesn't fill it.

Notable inclusions: Sexuality as its own slot (precedes the 2023 recap "questioning myself strongly sexually" line by ~10 months), Mexican-American as an identity bucket worth committing a stance on, Slave to responsibility as a self-named pattern, and Depression as something he expected to have a position on. The presence of "Idols" and "Meta-Thinking" foreshadows the and asking the right question later concept pages.

The "Sister" slot is the tell that this template was either inherited or aspirational — Yahir has only one brother.

Connections

  • piercing prompts — the genre
  • — the empty-skeleton subtype
  • end of chapter reflection — the impulse this prefigures
  • mexican american identity · depression · — concept buckets named here

Privacy

  • No PII or sensitive content (the slots are empty).
